design_philosophy.core_loop: Gather resources via mining, build fleet compositions, execute tactical combat in 3D space, accomplish mission objectives, hyperspace jump to next level with fleet persistence
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity vs fleet composition demands; tactical positioning in full 3D space; mothership vulnerability as immobile base
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a space fleet as exiled survivors reclaiming their homeworld; experience RTS gameplay in full three-dimensional combat rather than 2D constraints
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses 2D grid-based RTS conventions—forces players into true 3D tactical thinking with spherical play areas. Mothership immobility creates risk/reward tension unique to the genre. Fleet persistence across 16-mission campaign means strategic failures compound. Emphasizes formation-based group tactics over micro-management of individual units.

difficulty.lives_system: Mothership destruction ends campaign; persistent fleet carried between levels means cumulative strategic consequences
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpoint system; hyperspacing to next level saves fleet composition and resources
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can postpone hyperspace jump to gather more resources and build stronger fleet before advancing, providing self-regulation of difficulty pacing

progression.upgrade_system: Ship type unlocks via enemy capture, research vessel technology progression, or plot-driven discoveries. New unit types become available across 16 missions, expanding tactical options
progression.unlock_mechanism: Capturing enemy ships of new types unlocks them for construction; research vessel enables technology discoveries; story events unlock new ship variants
replayability_hooks: Choice between Kushan or Taiidan faction at campaign start (affects ship designs and specialized options)
Fleet composition decisions create different tactical approaches to same missions
Multiplayer modes for extended strategic play beyond campaign

world_building_techniques: Hand-drawn black-and-white cutscenes between each of 16 levels with narrative voiceovers
Story objectives that unfold and change during mission progression
Encounter-based narrative: pirates, mercenaries, traders, and rebels encountered sequentially reveal world state
Campaign narrative arc: Kushan exiles journey to reclaim ancient homeworld Hiigara from Taiidan Empire after planet Kharak's destruction

boss_design.philosophy: Strategic encounter design where enemy fleet compositions and formations present tactical challenges rather than single-entity boss fights; emphasis on adapting formation strategy to opponent fleet type

faction_indicators: Kushan vs Taiidan faction choice affects visual ship identity and specialized unit availability
Enemy faction encounters: Taiidan Empire (primary antagonist), pirates, mercenaries, traders, rebels suggest political/economic stratification
danger_signals: Mothership immobility creates permanent base vulnerability—player must defend stationary target
Resource depletion in levels creates survival pressure
Fleet losses persist across missions, creating strategic debt
environmental_storytelling: Stationary asteroids, gas clouds, dust clouds as resource nodes define economic geography of each level
3D spherical play areas with circular bisecting plane—spatial design encodes strategic positioning importance
Enemy ship variety and positioning suggests faction military doctrine and capability
